elements.sent-2411-001: Let $S$ be a probability measure-preserving invertible transformation (automorphism) of the space $(X, \mu)$, $f:X\to\Z$ be a $\mu$-measurable function.
elements.sent-2411-002: The cylindrical cascade $C: X\times \Z\to X\times \Z$ is defined by the formula \begin{equation}C(x,z)=\left(Sx, z+f(x)\right),\end{equation} it preserves the measure $\bar \mu =\mu\times\sharp$.
elements.sent-2411-003: Let $T_t$ be a flow preserving the measure $\mu$.
elements.sent-2411-004: The cylindrical flow $C_t: X\times \R\to X\times \R$ is defined similarly: \begin{equation}F_t(x,r)=\left(T_tx, r+ \sigma(t,x)\right),\end{equation} \begin{equation}\sigma(t,x):=\int_{0}^{t}f(T_sx)\,ds.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-005: It preserves the measure $\bar \mu =\mu\times m$, where $m$ is the Lebesgue measure on $\R$.
elements.sent-2411-006: Krygin and Atkinson \cite{Kr ,\cite{At independently proved that the cylindrical cascade with ergodic $S$ and integrable function $f:X\to\Z$ with zero mean are recurrent.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: This means that for any set $B\subset A$ of positive measure, for almost all points $x\in B$, the points $(x,z)$ return to $B\times \{z\}$ infinitely many times under the action of the cylindrical cascade.
elements.sent-2411-008: Since \begin{equation}C^n(x,z)=\left(Sx, z+\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}f(T^ix)\right),\end{equation} for Birkhoff sums, \begin{equation}\sum_{i=0}^{n_k-1}f(T^ix)=0\end{equation} for an infinite set of times $n_k$.
elements.sent-2411-009: Krygin formulated his result in the case when $S$ is an ergodic rotation, but in the proof he used only the ergodicity property of this automorphism.
elements.sent-2411-010: Atkinson noted this in the abstract of Zbl 0342.60049.
elements.sent-2411-011: In the case where $A$ is additionally a metric space and every open set has positive measure, then the Krygin-Atkinson theorem directly implies for almost all $x\in X$ the existence of a sequence $n_k\to\infty$ for which $T^{n_k x\to x$ and $\sum_{i=0 ^{n-1 f(T^ix)=0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: The case of flows is considered in the paper by Shneiberg \cite{Sh , in particular, he proved that for an ergodic flow $T_t: X\to X$ and $f:X\to\R$ with zero mean for almost all $x\in X$ there exists a sequence $n_k\to\infty$ \begin{equation}\sigma(t_k,x)=\int_0^{t_k} f(T_sx)\, ds =0.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-013: In the paper by Denisova \cite{De the question of when $\sigma(t,x)$ has infinitely many zeros $t_k\to\infty$ in each neighborhood of the point $x$ is studied.}
elements.sent-2411-014: Then for almost all $x$ such that $f(x)\neq 0$ there exists a sequence $t_k\to\infty$ such that $\sigma(t_k,x)=0$ and $T^{n_k x\to x$.\rm \bf Conjecture A.}
elements.sent-2411-015: Then for almost all $x\in A$ with $f(x)\neq 0$ there exists a sequence $t_k\to\infty$ such that $\sigma(t_k,x)=0$ and $T^{n_k x\in A$.\rm If the conjecture is true, then the following statement immediately follows.}
elements.sent-2411-016: Suppose that for a function $f:X\to\R$ with zero mean for almost all $x$ the integrals $\sigma(t,x)$ make sense and are continuous in $t$.
elements.sent-2411-017: Then for almost all $x$ with $f(x)\neq 0$ there is a sequence $t_k\to\infty$ such that $\sigma(t_k,x)=0$ and $T^{n_k x\to x$.\rm.}
elements.sent-2411-018: A measurable measure-preserving flow is isomorphic to a special flow $T_t$.
elements.sent-2411-019: We consider the case when the orbits have zero measure.
elements.sent-2411-020: The phase space $X$ for $T_t$ is the part of the plane under the graph of an integrable non-negative function $r: A\to \R^+$, $A\subset \R$.
elements.sent-2411-021: The metric on the phase space is the metric $\rho$ on the plane, and it is locally preserved by the flow.
elements.sent-2411-022: Recall that the point $x=(a,b)$ of the phase space moves vertically upward with a constant velocity: $T_t(a,b)=(a, b+t)$.
elements.sent-2411-023: The boundary of the phase space is glued in such a way that the points $(a, r(a))$ are identified with the points $(P(a),0)$, where $P$ is a given automorphism on $A$, the measurable domain of $r$.. \bf Theorem B.
elements.sent-2411-024: For a function $f:X\to\R$ with zero mean for almost all $x$, for $f(x)\neq 0$ there exists a sequence $t_k\to\infty$ such that $\sigma(t_k,x)=0$ and $T^{n_k x\to x$.\rm Proof.}
elements.sent-2411-025: The function $f$ is integrable on $X$, from Fubini's theorem it follows that the functions $F(s,x)= f(S_sx)$ for almost all $x$, as functions of $s$, are integrable on vertical segments with endpoints $(a,0)$ and $(a,r(a))$.
elements.sent-2411-026: Therefore, for almost all $x$, the indefinite integral \begin{equation}\Phi(t)= \int_0^{t} f(T_sx)\, ds\end{equation} is an absolutely continuous function on $\R$, hence for almost all $t$ there exists a limit \begin{equation}\lim_{\Delta \to 0}\frac {\int_t^{t+\Delta}f(T_sx)\, ds}{\Delta}=f(T_tx).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-027: But then for almost all $x$ there exists a limit \begin{equation}\lim_{ t\to 0}\frac {\int_0^{t}f(T_sx)\, ds}{t}=f(x).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-028: It follows from this that for almost all $x$ with $f(x)\neq 0$ there exists a number $\delta(x)>0$ such that \begin{equation}\int_0^{\delta(x)}f(T_sx)\, ds >f(T_tx)\delta(x)/2.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-029: We can obviously choose the function $\delta(x)$ in a measurable way, where $\delta(x)<\delta$ for a predetermined $\delta>0$.
elements.sent-2411-030: Let $U$ denote the intersection of the small disk with the set of $x$-s for which we have defined $\delta(x)$.
elements.sent-2411-032: In the phase space of a cylindrical flow, we consider the set \begin{equation}E=\{(x,h)\,:\, x\in U, f(x)>0, 0>h>-\delta(x)/4\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-033: Since the cilindrical flow $F_t$ is recurrent, for any $N$ there is $t>N$ \begin{equation}\bar\mu (F_tE\cap E)>0.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-034: Let \begin{equation}(T_tx,-h)\in F_tE\cap E, \ \ 0<h<\delta(x)/4,\end{equation} then by the continuity of the integral there is $\Delta(x,h)\in (0,\delta(x))$ such that \begin{equation}\int_0^{\Delta(x,h)}f(T_s T_tx)\, ds=h.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-035: This means that \begin{equation}\int_0^{t+\Delta(x,h)}f(T_s T_tx)\, ds=0,\end{equation} and by choice of $U$ the points $x$ and $T_{t+\Delta(x,h) x$ may be as close as we want.}
elements.sent-2411-036: Let's summarize the above in the following form.. \bf Lemma.
elements.sent-2411-037: A point $x$ is bad if there exists $N$ such that it is $N$-bad: for all $t>N$ from $\rho(T^{t_N x,x)<1/N$ it follows that $\sigma(t_N,x)\neq 0$.}
elements.sent-2411-038: If the measure of bad points is positive, then the measure of $N$-bad points is positive for some $N$.
elements.sent-2411-039: But by the lemma there is a point in it that is not $N$-bad.
elements.sent-2411-040: Thus, the set of bad points has measure 0.
elements.sent-2411-041: The theorem B is proved for points where $f(x)>0$, to finis the proof we reason symmetrically for $f(x)<0$.
elements.sent-2411-042: For an ergodic winding of the torus as $T_t$, we get the same with the same proof.
elements.sent-2411-043: If $f:X\to\Z$ has zero mean, then the cylindrical cascade $C: X\times \Z\to X\times \Z$, $ C(x,n)=\left(Sx, n+f(x)\right),$ is conservative.
elements.sent-2411-044: Let $x\in A$, $n(x)$ satisfy $S^{n(x)}\in A$, $S^k\notin A,$ for $ 0<k<n(x)$.. Put \begin{equation}\tilde Sx= S^{n(x)}x, \ \ \ \tilde f (x)=\sum_{i=0}^{n(x)-1}f(S^ix), \ x\in A.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-045: We have: $\tilde S:A\to A$ is ergodic on $A$ and $\int_A \tilde f (x)\, d\mu =0$.
elements.sent-2411-046: The induced cylindrical cascade $\tilde C:A\times\Z\to A\times \Z$ is recurrent, therefore so is the original cascade $C$.

elements.sent-2411-050: Let $n>2$, an $S^{\otimes n}$-invariant measure on the cube $X^{\times n}$ with projections $\mu^{\otimes 2}$ onto two-dimensional faces.
elements.sent-2411-051: The JR property for $S$ is that each such measure on $X^{\times n}$ is trivial, i.e. equal to $\mu^{\otimes n}$.
